description: Collomia linearis is a species of flowering plant in the phlox family known by the common names tiny trumpet and slenderleaf collomia. This tiny wildflower is native to North America, where it is fairly widespread across the north and west. It is an annual herb, rarely exceeding 30 centimetres (12 in) in height, with a velvety erect stem bearing long, narrow green leaves. Atop the stem is a cluster of up to 20 white or light pink flowers, each about a centimeter across.

common_problems: This phlox has better mildew resistance than P.
paniculata but it needs good air circulation and overhead watering should be avoided.
Root rot and spider mites can be an issue.
common_pests: Root rot and spider mites can be an issue.
care.used_for_details: (NC State ethnobotany) Infusion of root used as a wash by the Cherokees to make children grow and fatten.

description: Phlox paniculata is a species of flowering plant in the phlox family (Polemoniaceae). It is native to parts of the eastern and central United States. It is extensively cultivated in temperate regions as an ornamental plant and has become established in the wild in scattered locales in other regions. Common names include fall phlox, garden phlox, perennial phlox, summer phlox, and panicled phlox.
origin: Eastern United States

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)
care.used_for_details: (NC State ethnobotany) Leaf extract has been used as a laxative and for treating boils.

common_problems: Downy phlox has no serious insect or disease problems although hot, dry conditions can promote spider mite infestations.
It has resistance to powdery mildew.
common_pests: Downy phlox has no serious insect or disease problems although hot, dry conditions can promote spider mite infestations.
care.used_for_details: (NC State ethnobotany) Meskwaki Indians used an infusion of leaves to cure and purify the blood, and as a wash for eczema. They also used a compound containing the root used as a 'love medicine'.

care.toxicity_note: Non-toxic to Dogs, Non-Toxic to Cats, Non-Toxic to Horses.
common_problems: Moss phlox is relatively pest and disease-free and has moderate drought tolerance.
It is resistant to powdery mildew, unlike most other species of phlox.
Rabbits may feed on the foliage, but it is less palatable to deer.
Some cultivars have shown good resistance to phytophthora and southern blight.
common_pests: Moss phlox is relatively pest and disease-free and has moderate drought tolerance.
Spider mites can be a problem in hot, dry conditions, and nematodes can cause damage to the leaves in wet and humid conditions.
